nodes.name: Early end of the growing season

nodes.name: Cold temperatures at the beginning and end of the growing season

dry conditions in August also contributed to their formation

nodes.name: Warm and dry summers characterized by highly negative climate moisture indices

nodes.name: Provenances native to the ecozone in which the study site was located

source_spans.text: Local provenances showed the least amount of xylem anomalies
Local provenances showed the least amount of xylem anomalies

nodes.name: Provenances from the most northern ecozone

source_spans.text: Provenances from the most northern ecozone (Cold Boreal) were the most vulnerable to spring frosts
Provenances originating from southern ecozones showed a relatively low percentage of frost rings, but were susceptible to other xylem anomalies
Provenances originating from southern ecozones showed a relatively low percentage of frost rings, but were susceptible to other xylem anomalies
The latitude of the seed sources was negatively correlated with the intensity of light, blue, and double rings

nodes.name: End of the frost free period

nodes.name: Late end of the growing season

source_spans.text: Provenances that showed a higher percentage of blue rings usually came from areas with a late end of the growing season
xylem anomalies may serve as a sensitive early indicator of maladaptation to climate
evaluate the success of assisted migration in reforestation to address climate change
useful to monitor the health of natural populations
